---
title: "Week 1 Review Sheets — Start Here"
subtitle: "ENGR 102 Summer Bridge · Python Foundations & the Evidence Habit"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD — this page orients students to the sheet set.
Fill in prose where marked TODO. No secure quiz content anywhere;
use only generic examples. Scope: NO if/else, loops, lists, functions.
============================================================ -->

## What these sheets cover

<!-- TODO: 2–3 sentences. One continuous idea across Week 1:
     turn raw text readings into a trustworthy yes/no "record ready?" decision,
     and SHOW the evidence (predict / trace / test / debug / explain). -->

## How to read the bands

Every sheet uses the same three bands:

::: {.trap-band}
⚠️ **Watch out** — the classic mistake for this topic.
:::

::: {.check-band}
✅ **Non-mutating check** — how to inspect state without changing it.
:::

::: {.evidence-band}
🔎 **Show your evidence** — how to prove you know it (predict / trace / test / debug / explain).
:::

## The Week 1 pipeline (one line)

<!-- TODO: simple left-to-right diagram or sentence:
     text intake → cast to numbers → compute limits/gaps → boundary checks
     → combine with and/or/not → readiness decision → written closeout note -->

## Sheet index

| Sheet | Topic | Day |
|---|---|---|
| [Glossary](00_glossary.qmd) | Terms & symbols | — |
| [Output vs. Stored State](01_output_vs_state.qmd) | print vs. display vs. store | 1 |
| [Variables, Expressions, State](02_variables_expressions_state.qmd) | current vs. planned | 2 |
| [Types, Casting, Truthiness](03_types_casting_truthiness.qmd) | str/float/bool, casts | 3 |
| [Comparisons & Boundaries](04_comparisons_boundaries.qmd) | inclusive/exclusive edges | 4 |
| [Boolean Combinations](05_boolean_combinations.qmd) | and / or / not | 5 |
| [Traps & Evidence Habit](06_traps_and_evidence.qmd) | cross-cutting | all |
| [Synthesis / Exam-1 Practice](07_synthesis.qmd) | full pipeline | closeout |

## Scope guardrails (read once)

- Week 1 uses **only** variables, expressions, comparisons, and Boolean logic.
- **No** `if/else`, loops, lists, or functions — those come later.
- A right final answer is **not enough**: show the trace/test/debug/explanation.
